courses.title: Issues and Problems in American Society

notes: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.

Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .

Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer applicants must complete the articulated equivalents of the courses listed in the " Required Major Preparation Courses " section below, each with a grade of C (2.0) or better, no later than the end of the spring term prior to starting at UCSC in the fall. Winter Applicants Students transferring to UC Santa Cruz in the winter quarter are strongly advised to complete the articulated equivalents of the courses listed in the “ Recommended Preparation ” section below before transferring. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses prior to transfer will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Additional Information for Transfer Students are also encouraged to complete the California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring. For guidance on incorporating Cal-GETC into your academic plan, consult your community college counselor. Visit the Sociology website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Sociology B.A. Program Statement
